_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Review and approve data access requests, data sharing agreements, and data use authorizations for data assets within the assigned domain.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DG domain. DG-T-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-T-010 (DA, cosine: 0.435), TF-T-008 (TF, cosine: 0.428).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.635). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [authorization, manage, packages] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, agreements, approve, assets, assigned, authorizations] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.865 (P=0.890, R=0.842). P exceeds R by 0.048 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Manage authorization packages." and "Review and approve data access requests, data sharing agreements, and data use a" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.466 < STS 0.635 (gap -0.169). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [authorization, manage, packages] and [access, agreements, approve] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [authorization, manage, packages]. WIDAI terms: [access, agreements, approve, assets, assigned, authorizations].
